channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Memphis Grizzlies
channel.description: Memphis Grizzlies trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Grizzlies were going to pursue Ja Morant trade no matter the lottery outcome
item.description: Tim MacMahon: “I can just say the lottery results will have no impact on the approach moving forward with Ja Morant. They’re going to try to find a home for Ja Morant regardless of where they ended up in the lottery.”

item.title: It didn't take long for an agent on the ground in …
item.description: It didn't take long for an agent on the ground in Chicago to suggest to me that Memphis could potentially look to trade down from No. 3 as part of deal that ships out Morant, but I find such a scenario almost certainly too drastic. If anything Grizzlies president Zach Kleiman has a history of trading up and/or into the draft. Don't forget that he just did so last June, swapping picks with Portland to be able to select Cedric Coward at No. 11 overall.

item.title: Utah actually won the No. 3 pick after popping up …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Emerging from the sequestered lottery room to report Utah actually won the No. 3 pick after popping up second as well. So, the league had to toss out that outcome and draw again… which led to the Grizzlies claiming the third pick.

item.title: Wizards win 2026 draft lottery
item.description: Shams Charania: Top 4 order in the 2026 NBA Draft: 1. Washington Wizards 2. Utah Jazz 3. Memphis Grizzlies 4. Chicago Bulls

item.title: Wizards, Jazz, Grizzlies and Bulls make the top four in the draft lottery
item.description: Josh Robbins: The Wizards, Jazz, Grizzlies and Bulls are the teams that will draft somewhere in the top four.

item.title: Brandon Clarke dead at 29 years old
item.description: Michael Scotto: Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke has passed away at 29 years old. Clarke was a first-round draft pick in 2019 and was an All-Rookie First Team selection. He averaged 10.2 points and 5.5 rebounds in seven NBA seasons for Memphis. Thoughts and prayers to his loved ones 🙏🏼

item.title: Statement on the passing of Brandon Clarke
item.description: Priority Sports: Statement on the passing of Brandon Clarke.

item.title: During this difficult time, the family kindly asks for …
item.description: Priority Sports: During this difficult time, the family kindly asks for privacy as they grieve and make arrangements. Your thoughts, prayers, and understanding are greatly appreciated.

item.title: Grizzlies release statement on tragic passing of Brandon Clarke
item.description: Memphis Grizzlies: Another statement on the passing of Brandon Clarke.

item.title: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver issued the following …
item.description: NBA Communications: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver issued the following statement today regarding the passing of Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke.

item.title: Brandon Clarke's death being investigated as possible drug overdose
item.description: Brandon Clarke's death is being investigated as a possible overdose ... with officials finding drug paraphernalia inside the home the Memphis Grizzlies player was staying at on Monday. The Los Angeles Fire Department responded to a medical emergency call around 5 PM on Monday ... and when paramedics got to the scene, Clarke was declared deceased, according to NBC4.

item.title: In 2019 we headed to Summer League, and the kid out of …
item.description: Chris Vernon: in 2019 we headed to Summer League, and the kid out of Gonzaga they drafted 21st overall won the MVP. He became beloved from literally the first time he wore the uniform. Look up and 7 years later he is gone. Still can’t believe it. So intensely sad. RIP BC 😢

item.title: Ja Morant remembers Brandon Clarke

item.title: NBPA releases statement on the passing of Brandon Clarke
item.description: The NBPA: Statement from NBPA Executive Director Andre Iguodala and NBPA President Fred VanVleet on the tragic passing of Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke.

item.title: "He was an extended brother."
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "He was an extended brother." Danny Green and the NBA Today crew took a moment to share a message on Brandon Clarke.

item.title: Spoke with Brandon Clarke's mentor and former coach …
item.description: Drew Hill: Spoke with Brandon Clarke's mentor and former coach Tony Darden. The AZ basketball community is crushed. Tony shared these videos of his son AJ, who Brandon treated like a little brother. The first from draft night in 2019. "It was really hard to break the news"

item.title: Spurs had a moment of silence for Brandon Clarke and …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Spurs had a moment of silence for Brandon Clarke and Jason Collins. Keldon Johnson came into the NBA at the same time as Clarke. "He seemed like an amazing person and I always heard great things about him," Keldon told me. "It sucks. Sending condolences to his family."

item.title: NBC inexplicably faded to black then cut to commercial …
item.description: The Spurs announcer asks for a moment of silence for the recent passing of Brandon Clarke and Jason Collins. NBC inexplicably faded to black then cut to commercial in the middle of the moment; a DraftK*ngs commercial played on the broadcast.

item.title: “I’m walking with a GOAT right here,” Morant said in …
item.description: In another post, Morant shared a photo of himself with Clarke and Jaren Jackson Jr. Morant later shared a post from Grizzlies social media manager Devin Walker that featured him with Clarke during the 2019 NBA Summer League when he won MVP. “I’m walking with a GOAT right here,” Morant said in the video as the pair walked off the court.

item.title: “Just a really good person, a really good teammate, …
item.description: On Tuesday afternoon, Minnesota Timberwolves forward Kyle Anderson was one of several former and current Memphis players who paid tribute to Clarke. “Just a really good person, a really good teammate, obviously someone who was really good on the court. Just someone I could instantly see and we would start laughing and start sharing jokes. He was Memphis’ own, for sure. He did his part in the community,” Anderson told The Athletic. “It’s just crazy. It really don’t make no sense. I was on the pho

item.title: “He was an incredible player, but an even better …
item.description: Clarke had one year remaining on his contract with Memphis, where he had established himself as a beloved teammate. Niele Ivey, a former Grizzlies assistant coach who is now the head coach of Notre Dame’s women’s basketball team, told The Athletic that she was “heartbroken” to hear of Clarke’s death. “He was an incredible player, but an even better person,” she said. “I have nothing but great memories of his positive energy, enthusiasm and the way he uplifted everyone around him — teammates and

item.title: Jaren Jackson Jr on Brandon Clarke: I couldn’t imagine …
item.description: Jaren Jackson Jr on Brandon Clarke: I couldn’t imagine a world where this was even a possibility. i love you forever brother, this is absolutely devastating. i just wish i could talk to you. you were so much to so many and even more to me . i will never have the words to describe what this feels like . i still can’t believe this . to your family and friends and everyone you were always a light and you were always so genuine and real and our friendship went far beyond anything on the court . i tr

item.title: “I’ve worked out with Memphis and Boston. ... Great …
item.description: Scott Richey: “I’ve worked out with Memphis and Boston. ... Great workouts. Great feedback. I think it's all about evaluating my options and seeing what's the best fit." — @IlliniMBB guard Andrej Stojakovic (@AndrejSto2) on his pre-draft process so far. #Illini #NBADraft

item.title: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa met with the Wizards in …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa met with the Wizards in Chicago during the NBA pre-draft camp. Washington is supposed to visit with “multiple players” in DC for the top pick, source days. Dybantsa also met with Utah, Chicago, Sacramento, Brooklyn, Atlanta, Dallas, Memphis & the Clippers.

item.title: Sitting courtside at Oak Ridge to watch the Ones …
item.description: Jason Beede: Sitting courtside at Oak Ridge to watch the Ones Basketball League is former #Magic and current Grizzlies guard Kentavious Caldwell-Pope. @OBLhoops

item.title: The NBA, of course, continues to target the 2027-28 …
item.description: Orlando and Memphis split this season's two Europe games in Berlin in London. Berlin and Paris are scheduled to serve as host cities for the two-game tour in 2028. The NBA, of course, continues to target the 2027-28 season as its launch date for a 12-to-16 team NBA Europe circuit that would be separate from #thisleague. Wemby and the Spurs played two games against Indiana in Paris in January 2025 on the NBA's last in-season visit to France.

item.title: NBA player anonymous poll names Memphis the least desired NBA destination
item.description: When players were asked which team would be at the top of their “no-trade” list, Memphis received more than three times as many votes as the second-place Washington Wizards. "I'll play anywhere, honestly,” one player explained. “But I would have to say Memphis is a place I don't want to live. So that would be the reason why I wouldn't want to play there.” Said another player: “It has nothing to do with the team. It's the location of the team."

item.title: You used to have just the hottest babes in the front …
item.description: Tony Allen: "I'm going to tell you what put a little flame to it. I remember this vividly. I'll tell you what put a little flame. Well, put a little flame to it. You're married now and we can speak on this. You're married now. You used to have just the hottest babes in the front court." Chandler Parsons: "Why was that a bad thing?" Tony Allen: "And then it was that was the focus and then you're rehab, you're rehab somewhere in LA. We look up you in Cabo. It was kind of weird and then it was coac

item.title: Chandler Parsons on Memphis experience: 'I felt like I was in a constant battle with my own home fans and my own front office'
item.description: Chandler Parsons: I appreciate you saying that because again, the shit I had to go through with my injuries and my own personal struggle to get back and to be the player I once was was so difficult. And then you add this pressure of expectation, a big contract, being a missing piece and failing at all of those. It sucked and I just felt not attacked but just I felt like I was in a constant battle with my own home fans and my own front office, my own ownership almost blaming me for taking this co

item.title: Cooper Flagg, Dylan Harper, Kon Knueppel headline All-Rookie First Team
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Rookie First Team: Cedric Coward, @memgrizz VJ Edgecombe, @sixers Cooper Flagg, @dallasmavs Dylan Harper, @spurs Kon Knueppel, @hornets

item.title: “I appreciate it. It’s been rough. But at the same …
item.description: – I’m sorry about the news with Brandon’s passing. How have you been holding up with everything? Cedric Coward: “I appreciate it. It’s been rough. But at the same time, I’m grateful for the time that I had with him. Now you honor his memory by winning games and doing what he was doing with being a great teammate.”

item.title: He didn’t treat me like the stereotypical vet, giving …
item.description: – What made Brandon such a great teammate? Cedric Coward: “I only had a year with him. But at the same time, it was so impactful due to his love for his teammates. Especially for me as a rookie, he had a lot of love for me. He didn’t treat me like the stereotypical vet, giving me the typical rookie treatment. He did at times, but it was all fun and love. So with him, I thought it was so inspirational that he dealt with so many injuries, but he came in every day with a smile on his face. He came

item.title: “It’s about doing what we can to get better each and …
item.description: – In the spirit of you wanting to honor Brandon’s memory by winning games, how do you go about that? Cedric Coward: “It’s about doing what we can to get better each and every day. He was super impactful, especially during the times that the Grizzlies made the playoffs. So it’s about getting back there. Getting back to the playoffs is huge. It’s also about not taking every day for granted. That’s the biggest thing. You have to understand that time is short, not just for the people that you’re wit

item.title: “Yeah, I’ve talked to KD a lot. After games, I talk to …
item.description: – You’ve also said you hope to ask questions to some of the other star vets, including LeBron [James], Steph [Curry] and KD. Have you been able to do that yet? Cedric Coward: “Yeah, I’ve talked to KD a lot. After games, I talk to him a lot. I haven’t talked to ‘Bron or Steph yet. But the opportunities will come. I was born believing that closed mouths don’t get fed. So whenever I get those opportunities, best believe that I’m going to talk a lot to them about it. But at the end of the day, it’ll

item.title: Patrick St. Andrews joins Bucks as an assistant coach
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Milwaukee Bucks will add Patrick St. Andrews as an assistant coach, reuniting him with coach Taylor Jenkins. He was an assistant coach for the Blazers. Prior, he was an assistant coach with Jenkins on the Grizzlies and with Mike Budenholzer on the Bucks and Hawks.

item.title: Jovan Buha: “I think there will be competition for …
item.description: Jovan Buha: “I think there will be competition for (Austin Reaves) services. If I'm Brooklyn or Chicago, I think he's, I mean, Brooklyn drafted a million point guards and ball handlers last year, but I don't think any of them project to be as good as Austin Reeves currently is. I think the sweet spot is probably going to be around five years, 200 million. But if you get that number down to around 35 million average annual, that's where I could see someone, Brooklyn, Chicago, Memphis, come in and

item.title: Former Memphis Grizzlies and Sacramento Kings head …
item.description: Peter Hooley: Former Memphis Grizzlies and Sacramento Kings head coach, Dave Joerger, has been interviewed by Melbourne United about their vacant head coaching position Joerger has a strong relationship with Joe Ingles and is currently assistant at the Milwaukee Bucks.

item.title: Dave Joerger in talks to coach Australian team
item.description: Melbourne United are the last team in the League looking for a head coach, and Hools is Hearing that a very prominent and well-respected NBA name is at the top of the list. Current Milwaukee Bucks assistant Dave Joerger has been interviewed by the club recently about potentially taking over after Dean Vickerman’s departure. The 52-year-old has previously been Head Coach of the Memphis Grizzlies and Sacramento Kings, before being added to the Bucks staff two years ago.

item.title: He's the Spur w/at least 13 consecutive points in a …
item.description: Michael C. Wright: Julian Champagnie scored 13 straight from 8:21 to 4:38 in 1Q. He's the Spur w/at least 13 consecutive points in a playoff game since Kawhi Leonard scored 16 straight in Game 4 of the 1st Rd against Memphis in 2017, per ESPN Research.

item.description: Damichael Cole: Some Grizzlies draft workouts today, per league sources: J’Vonne Hadley (Louisville); Sam Alexis (Indiana); Chase Ross (Marquette): Tyler Tanner (Vanderbilt)

item.title: On average, top-five picks from 2000 through 2019 …
item.description: The dream of a decade-plus of production for a franchise cornerstone also doesn't materialize very often. On average, top-five picks from 2000 through 2019 lasted 5.1 seasons with their original team, with 56% lasting until their fifth year (and their second contract) and 44% leaving before then. Only 6% played at least 10 seasons with their initial team. (Tatum will bump that figure to 7% next season.) And for now, only five of the 100 top-five picks in that period are still with the team that

item.title: On Ja’s IG story…
item.description: Mark Jones: On Ja’s IG story…

item.title: The NBA's Board of Governors vote passed 29-1 -- with …
item.description: Shams Charania: The NBA's Board of Governors vote passed 29-1 -- with the Memphis Grizzlies as the lone vote against the new anti-tanking lottery reform, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Grizzlies opposed lottery reform over rule barring teams from three consecutive top-5 picks
item.description: Shams Charania: Sources said the Grizzlies voted against the new lottery reform solely because of the new rule disallowing a team from picking top 5 in 3 consecutive years. As @BobbyMarks42 says, the Grizzlies have the more favorable 2027 first-rounder of Cleveland, Minnesota and Utah, and because the Jazz again have a top-5 pick this year, their first in 2027 cannot be in the top-5 under new rules.

item.title: As I currently understand it, if the Jazz's 2027 …
item.description: Jake Fischer: As I currently understand it, if the Jazz's 2027 first-rounder that's owed to Memphis, and can't be top-five next year, were to land at No. 5, it would be slated at No. 6. But, there's also the possibility that the league would just discard that outcome and draw again for No. 5.

item.title: According to NBA president of league operations Byron …
item.description: According to NBA president of league operations Byron Spruell, Grizzlies general manager Zach Kleiman and team president Jason Wexler were both heavily involved in the process. Kleiman played an active role in the league’s general manager committee discussions, while Wexler participated in Board of Governors meetings, where Memphis ultimately became the lone organization to vote against the proposal.

item.title: "If you were to grandfather in already traded picks, …
item.description: While the reform is designed to further discourage tanking and flatten lottery odds across the league, Memphis appears to be the franchise most directly affected by one of its unintended consequences. Evan Wasch, NBA executive vice president, head of basketball strategy and growth, explained how the restrictions with the Utah pick travels to the acquiring team in the new system. The league didn't want to create an imbalance in value on traded and non-traded picks. "If you were to grandfather in

item.title: As multiple general managers told The Athletic long …
item.description: To use the Memphis example above, some asked why the restriction wouldn’t be that Utah couldn’t land in the top five in 2027 regardless of whose pick it owned. Surely the rule was never designed to stop the Grizzlies from picking there. As multiple general managers told The Athletic long before this rule was adopted, however, this created asymmetries in the market that likely would have resulted in teams of roughly equal talent just trading picks with each other. Teams could also sidestep trade

item.title: NBAers in for Canada this summer:
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: To sum up NBAers in for Canada this summer: SGA, NAW, Barrett, Brooks, Dort, Nembhard bros, Mathurin, Olynyk, George, Miller Out this summer but in for this cycle: Edey, Powell Didn’t commit: Murray, Sharpe, Wiggins Committed but not selected: Lawson, Banton, Houstan, Prosper

item.title: As for Domantas Sabonis ... The Kings' former All-Star …
item.description: As for Domantas Sabonis ... The Kings' former All-Star center is another name, behind Antetokounmpo and Memphis' Ja Morant, that league executives are projecting to be made available once again as part of this summer's trade landscape. Sacramento is known to be looking to shed some significant salary if it can this offseason and seems likely to field more interest in Sabonis than other veterans.

item.title: So yeah, just stuff like that. You got me. I told my …
item.description: Any rookie or 'welcome to the NBA' moments for you? VJ Edgecombe: When I was a primary defender for Ja Morant, he had 40... Yeah, I say that, I saw like that move of James Harden. You'd be like, 'Man, I won't foul him. If he does that, I won't foul him.' I fell for that, like he put my hand right in the cookie jar. So yeah, just stuff like that. You got me. I told my teammates before the game, I'm like, 'I won't get caught with that.' Go right there. Beep. Foul.

item.title: Already shot this one down a few weeks back, but a …
item.description: Gerald Bouquet: Seeing the Ja Morant-to-Phoenix talk heating up again. Already shot this one down a few weeks back, but a source reiterated today that the Suns are not interested in trading for Morant

item.title: Dallin Hall out of Virginia will workout with the …
item.description: Drew Hill: 6’4 PG Dallin Hall out of Virginia will workout with the Grizzlies today, his agent Jake Cohen of Iconic Sports Management tells me. Hall was one of the better playmakers in the country last season, ranking 4th in D1 in AST Ratio (per 800 min) and Top 15 in AST/ TO ratio.

item.title: Reaching inside, they found seven smaller bags …
item.description: Six weeks before he died, Brandon Clarke sat in the back seat of a deputy’s patrol car, handcuffs digging into his left wrist as he shifted his 6-foot-8 frame in the cramped space. Outside, three officers searched his white Corvette along the shoulder of Highway 64 in eastern Arkansas. Across the two-lane road sat a steakhouse serving ribeye and deep-fried alligator, its empty parking lot waiting for the dinner regulars. Moments earlier, deputies had pulled over the Memphis Grizzlies forward wit

item.title: The bags were labeled “100% Pure Mitragyna Speciosa,” …
item.description: The bags were labeled “100% Pure Mitragyna Speciosa,” with handwritten names such as “The Juice,” “Euflooria” and “Gold Rhino.” Online, the various strains totaled $223 and promised a “solid amount of relief,” “a speed boost” and “a mind and body melting burst of happiness in a plant.” Waiving his Miranda rights, Clarke quickly identified what had caught officers’ attention. “It’s just kratom,” he said. In the same botanical family as coffee, kratom has been used for centuries in Southeast Asia.

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks will add Vince Legarza & Brad Jones as …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Milwaukee Bucks will add Vince Legarza & Brad Jones as assistant coaches. Legarza was an assistant with Mike Budenholzer on the Suns & Bucks. He & Taylor Jenkins both worked together under Budenholzer on the Hawks. Jones was an assistant for Jenkins on the Grizzlies.

item.title: Greater Memphis Chamber Interim President and CEO …
item.description: Greater Memphis Chamber Interim President and CEO Duncan Williams recently said the Memphis Grizzlies are here to stay for "multiple decades" to come. During the annual Mid-Year Chairman's Forum June 9 at The Guest House at Graceland in Memphis, Williams relayed the "message" from his June 9 meeting with Grizzlies leadership, which he said included team president Jason Wexler. "The message is this, it's not a matter of if they are going to sign, it's when. It's I's and T's at this point, people,

item.title: AJ Dybantsa visiting Wizards, Jazz next week?
item.description: Dybantsa is the projected No. 1 pick and will visit both the Wizards and Jazz next week. Typically, teams bring in their top option as close to the draft as possible to leave a lasting good impression and to prevent leaks from within the organization or on the player’s side. Dybantsa will only meet with the top two teams and will not travel to Memphis (third pick) or Chicago (fourth pick).

item.title: Heat eyeing Trae Young as fallback if Giannis Antetokounmpo pursuit falls short?
item.description: I've been told by various sources that — in addition to the LA Clippers' Kawhi Leonard and Memphis' Ja Morant — Wizards point guard Trae Young has emerged as another "big fish" backup option for the Miami Heat in the event that Boston, Portland or some other team manages to outbid Pat Riley and Co. in the trade chase for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: Different possibilities are being discussed, including …
item.description: Among the most interesting outcomes that will affect the rest of the NBA will be how an expansion draft is structured -- the last time was in 2004, and it was for just one team, the Charlotte Bobcats. At that time, teams were able to protect eight players and lose only one. There is also the question of how the league will realign the conferences. Different possibilities are being discussed, including moving one team from the Western Conference to the East -- with Memphis, Minnesota and New Orle

item.title: Forward Baba Miller is in Memphis.
item.description: Damichael Cole: Forward Baba Miller is in Memphis. Projected to go early in the second round

item.title: Bennett Stirtz tells me he will be attending the NBA …
item.description: Elliot Clough: NEW: Bennett Stirtz tells me he will be attending the NBA Draft at the Barclays Center in Brooklyn, New York next Tuesday. Coach Ben McCollum will join Stirtz and his family in the green room. So far, Stirtz has worked out with the following teams: Charlotte Hornets, Chicago Bulls, Dallas Mavericks, Memphis Grizzlies, Oklahoma City Thunder, Toronto Raptors. He'll also workout for the Golden State Warriors prior to the draft.

item.title: Stirtz has worked out with the following teams:
item.description: Elliot Clough: Bennett Stirtz tells me he will be attending the NBA Draft at the Barclays Center in Brooklyn, New York next Tuesday. Coach Ben McCollum will join Stirtz and his family in the green room. So far, Stirtz has worked out with the following teams: Charlotte Hornets Chicago Bulls Dallas Mavericks Memphis Grizzlies Oklahoma City Thunder Toronto Raptors He'll also workout for the Golden State Warriors prior to the draft.

item.title: Though Thibodeau didn’t respond to The Athletic’s …
item.description: Though Thibodeau didn’t respond to The Athletic’s message seeking comment, one of his favorite players, Taj Gibson, told SiriusXM NBA Radio that he had spoken to the coach and that “he didn’t have any kind of malice in his heart. He didn’t have any kind of hatred. He was so happy for the guys. He was just really so proud of the guys and what they accomplished.”

item.title: Hawks hope to re-sign center Jock Landale
item.description: In addition, the Hawks hope to retain center Landale in free agency. Landale spent this past season with the Memphis Grizzlies and Hawks, averaging career-highs in points (10.6), rebounds (5.7), and minutes (22.1), while shooting an efficient 51.5 percent from the field and 38.3 percent beyond the arc. Numerous teams are expected to express interest in the 30-year-old center.

item.title: Morant. Amick: Ja Morant, I mean, the only two teams …
item.description: Lou Williams: I’m going to throw some names out there and you give me your guess on where they'll be next year. All right? Sam Amick: Yes, sir. Williams: Let's start with Ja Morant. Amick: Ja Morant, I mean, the only two teams I've heard and I'll pick one of them is New Orleans and Sacramento, so I'll probably push it to New Orleans way. Chandler Parsons: I'm going to throw Minnesota out there just for fun. Chandler Parsons: I'm going to throw Minnesota out there just for fun. Amick: Yeah, that

item.title: The Grizzlies are hiring Mike Curtis as the new head …
item.description: Damichael Cole: News: The Grizzlies are hiring Mike Curtis as the new head of player health and performance. Most recently at Virginia, he notably spent six seasons with the Grizzlies from 2002-08 as the strength and conditioning coach.

item.title: Kentavious Caldwell-Pope exercises $21.6 million player option
item.description: Shams Charania: Memphis Grizzlies guard Kentavious Caldwell-Pope is exercising his $21.6 million player option for the 2026-27 season, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Grizzlies hopeful Ja Morant becomes Plan B for teams missing out on Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: In Memphis, Ja Morant was once the superstar whose uncertain future we analyzed at every turn. And then, somewhere along the line, the noise around him went quiet. Yet league sources say the Grizzlies are hopeful that Morant will be some team’s Plan B when the dust settles on Antetokounmpo’s situation. The Heat, in particular, have long been known to have serious interest in the 26-year-old, two-time All-Star. The thinking, it seems, is that Miami’s potential package might reset the market that

item.title: Sam Amick on Ja Morant: "The only two teams I have …
item.description: Sam Amick on Ja Morant: "The only two teams I have heard and I will pick one of them is New Orleans and Sacramento. I will probably push it the New Orleans way."

item.title: Not much has changed for Cameron Boozer as he enters …
item.description: Not much has changed for Cameron Boozer as he enters what should be a straightforward draft night. Sources say he visited Memphis last week and previously visited Chicago and Utah, covering all bases other than Washington. Although he is under consideration in Utah, where his father is part of the front office, rival teams believe the Jazz will have a hard time passing on Dybantsa or Peterson.

item.title: While the Grizzlies weigh trade offers for Ja Morant …
item.description: Jake Fischer: While the Grizzlies weigh trade offers for Ja Morant and the decision at No. 3, I’ve also heard several teams have expressed interest in trading for Euroleague star Tarik Biberovic, whose draft rights are held by the Grizzlies. Biberovic, 25, shot 44% from distance for Fenerbahce

item.title: Darryn Peterson said he had no communication with …
item.description: Drew Hill: Darryn Peterson said he had no communication with Memphis following the combine/lottery in Chicago.

item.title: Charlotte, armed with picks Nos. 14 and 18, is …
item.description: Charlotte, armed with picks Nos. 14 and 18, is believed to be exploring avenues to move up, sources said, along with Chicago at No. 15 (which also owns the No. 4 pick) and Memphis at No. 16 (which also owns the 3rd overall selection).

item.title: They have discussed a variety of trade-back scenarios, …
item.description: The Mavericks are expected to go into next season with Flagg and Kyrie Irving as cornerstones. They control the Nos. 9, 30 and 48 selections in this week’s NBA Draft. They have discussed a variety of trade-back scenarios, including one, according to league sources, that would net Dallas the No. 16 pick that Memphis currently controls.

item.title: Wolves not interested in Ja Morant
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Wolves have not been in the Ja Morant mix. Decision was made to look elsewhere for backcourt help

item.title: Timberwolves not targeting Ja Morant
item.description: The Wolves are not pursuing Memphis point guard Ja Morant, according to team sources, but will continue to canvas the league for other possibilities.

item.title: Pelicans have no plans to pursue Ja Morant
item.description: The Pelicans have been mentioned at various points this offseason as a potential Ja Morant trade suitor, but The Stein Line has been advised that New Orleans has no plans to pursue Memphis' very available guard. The Pelicans, as one source put it, drafted Jeremiah Fears this time last year to be their point guard of the future. Thus they are not, we're told, plotting any sort of run at Morant. New Orleans, however, does continue to receive significant external trade interest in Murphy and Herb J

item.title: While the Grizzlies weigh trade offers for Ja Morant …
item.description: Jake Fischer: While the Grizzlies weigh trade offers for Ja Morant and the decision at No. 3, I’ve also heard several teams have expressed interest in trading for Euroleague star Tarik Biberovic, whose draft rights are held by the Grizzlies. Biberovic, 25, shot 44% from distance for Fenerbahce over the past three seasons and led them to the 2025 Euroleague title.

item.title: Cedric Coward: "Memphis has gotten a bad rap"
item.description: HH: With this young core and picks No. 3 and 16, where do you see this team’s trajectory? Cedric Coward: 'I see a team that’s trying to develop talent where we can compete with the Spurs, the Thunder, and teams of that caliber that are championship-level teams. For me, I can focus on what I do on the court on a day-to-day basis and how I treat my own work in terms of making sure I’m on top of it and then that will trickle down to helping me be a leader and making sure they’re putting in their ow

item.title: Cedric Coward on Ja Morant: "He’s one of my favorite teammates I’ve ever been around"
item.description: HH: How would you describe Ja Morant, and what could be next for his future? Coward: 'He’s one of my favorite teammates I’ve ever been around. He’s just a standup guy. He’s one of them dudes. When we had the ice storm, he offered to pay for my groceries. I declined it, but the offer was just who he is. He always wants to take care of people. He took care of me as a rookie in certain situations. He’s a good person to be around. He’ll joke around with you. Usually, when you go to teams with supers

item.title: Kings unlikely to pursue Ja Morant, plan to draft point guard
item.description: With the Kings projected to take a point guard with the No. 7 pick in the NBA Draft, the chances of the franchise pursuing Ja Morant going forward are unlikely, HoopsHype has learned. This past season, the Kings were willing to acquire Morant if they were incentivized, but Memphis was uninterested in giving up draft compensation to move Morant, a two-time All-Star.

item.title: Listen, a lot of these guys and these prospects are …
item.description: Kendrick Perkins: "I like Memphis. I actually love Memphis. I love the city of Memphis. I don't even want to get into it with the Memphians and all that stuff, man. I got people out there, I was going to school there. I'm just telling you what I'm hearing, so they don't be on my ass like back pockets. Listen, a lot of these guys and these prospects are trying to avoid getting drafted by Memphis. I'm just telling you this, real talk. What y'all thoughts on this? I'm just telling you, this is some

item.title: Memphis takes Cameron Boozer at No. 3
item.description: Jake Fischer: Cam Boozer will be the selection for Memphis at No. 3, sources say.

item.title: Cam Boozer landed a shoe deal with Jordan Brand …
item.description: Nick DePaula: Cam Boozer landed a shoe deal with Jordan Brand earlier this year, and is already featured in the roll out for the upcoming Jordan Triangle performance model.

item.title: It's my moment, but it's our moment as a family."
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "I don't even know how to describe it honestly, man. ... It's my moment, but it's our moment as a family." Cameron Boozer reacts to being drafted No. 3 overall to the Memphis Grizzlies 🐻

item.title: Cameron Boozer on next season with Memphis: 'We're going to surprise a lot of people'
item.description: Damichael Cole: Cameron Boozer said he’s excited to join the young core in Memphis. He mentioned Cedric Coward by name. “I think we’re going to surprise alot of people this year.”

item.title: Bennett Stirtz heads to OKC at No. 16 after trade with Memphis
item.description: Shams Charania: Sources: Memphis is trading No. 16 pick Bennett Stirtz to the Oklahoma City Thunder for two second-round picks.

item.title: Memphis Grizzlies will select Bennett Stirtz with the …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Memphis Grizzlies will select Bennett Stirtz with the No. 16 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: Memphis will draft No. 17 now via OKC.
item.description: Shams Charania: Memphis will draft No. 17 now via OKC. So the Grizzlies get two second-round picks and swap the No. 16 pick for No. 17.

item.title: Memphis is trading No. 17 to the Detroit Pistons to …
item.description: Shams Charania: Another Grizzlies trade: Memphis is trading No. 17 to the Detroit Pistons to move back to No. 21 -- for three second round picks.

item.title: The Grizzlies moved back from No. 16 to No. 17, then …
item.description: Shams Charania: To recap: the Grizzlies moved back from No. 16 to No. 17, then No. 17 to No. 21 -- accumulating five second-round picks to move back just five spots.

item.title: They've got 14 second-round picks now
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Grizzlies net 5 second-round picks thus far by moving down from 16th to 21st in two trades. They've got 14 second-round picks now.

item.title: Karim Lopez heads to Memphis at No. 21
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Memphis Grizzlies will select Karim Lopez with the No. 21 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft. This pick was acquired in a trade earlier tonight.

item.title: “Now that it’s done, it’s a blessing.”
item.description: Damichael Cole: Karim Lopez said he worked out with the Grizzlies a couple of weeks ago. Zach Kleiman also went down to Australia and saw him play earlier this season. “Now that it’s done, it’s a blessing.”

item.title: Asked Zach Kleiman about Ja Morant. He said he's …
item.description: Jonah Dylan: Asked Zach Kleiman about Ja Morant. He said he's focused on the draft and "we're not going to speculate on hypothetical transactions tonight."

item.title: Kendrick Perkins: Teams were asking Memphis to attach …
item.description: Kendrick Perkins: Teams were asking Memphis to attach picks in order to take Ja Morant, not the other way around. They were asking Memphis, "Hey man, attach picks with Ja and we'll take him."

item.title: Grizzlies actively fielding offers to trade the No. 32 pick in the draft
item.description: Shams Charania: Memphis, like the Knicks at No. 31, is actively fielding offers to move the No. 32 pick for Night 2 of the draft, per sources. So picks 31 and 32 are up for auction in trades around the NBA -- with future assets being proposed.

item.title: If Memphis doesn’t trade tonight’s No. 32 pick, I’ve …
item.description: Jake Fischer: If Memphis doesn’t trade tonight’s No. 32 pick, I’ve heard they hold some interest in Aaron Nkrumah. But the Grizzlies are indeed seeking more future second-round draft capital. Memphis already netted five second-round picks by maneuvering down the board last night. The Grizzlies, sources say, also received a 2029 most favorable second of Detroit/New York/Milwaukee, a 2031 Dallas second, and a 2032 Detroit second, for sliding from No. 16 to No. 21.

item.title: The Detroit Pistons traded the No. 21 pick (Karim …
item.description: Michael Scotto: FULL TRADE: The Detroit Pistons traded the No. 21 pick (Karim Lopez) and three-second round picks (a 2029 second that’s the second-best of Pistons, Bucks, Knicks, a 2031 Mavericks second, and a 2032 Pistons second) to the Memphis Grizzlies for pick 17 (Ebuka Okorie), per sources.

item.title: Xavier Tillman signs with Turkish team
item.description: Xavier Tillman is continuing his basketball career overseas. The former Boston Celtics, Memphis Grizzlies, and Charlotte Hornets center has signed with Turkish club Trabzonspor, the organization announced on Wednesday.

item.title: One team source indicated that the Warriors had …
item.description: One team source indicated that the Warriors had Lendeborg rated about even with Burries and only had Duke’s Cam Boozer clearly placed on a higher level in this draft.

item.title: Memphis picks Richie Saunders at No. 32
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Memphis Grizzlies will select Richie Saunders with the No. 32 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: While the Grizzlies fielded a ton of trade interest in …
item.description: Jake Fischer: While the Grizzlies fielded a ton of trade interest in No. 32, Memphis plans to keep BYU guard Richie Saunders, sources say.

item.title: Grizzlies are really tight on roster spots. Something …
item.description: Keith Smith: Grizzlies are really tight on roster spots. Something is going to have to give with guys moving on at some point. I wonder if Richie Saunders ends up a domestic draft-and-stash and starts his career in the G League, as he recovers from the torn ACL he suffered last season.

item.title: Isaiah Stewart traded to Memphis
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: The Detroit Pistons are trading C/F Isaiah Stewart to the Memphis Grizzlies for three future second-round picks, sources tell ESPN. Pistons clear Stewart's salary and pick up assets while Grizzlies acquire a 25-year-old as a rugged frontcourt piece.

item.title: Memphis is trading these same three second-round picks …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Memphis is trading these same three second-round picks that the Grizzlies received from the Pistons last night, sources say, back to Detroit, in order to land Isaiah Stewart.

item.title: The three picks they’re getting back are the three …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Source confirms the Pistons have traded Isaiah Stewart, their longest-tenured player, to the Grizzlies. The three picks they’re getting back are the three they traded last night (2029 2nd that’s the second-best of Pistons, Knicks and Bucks, a 2031 Mavs 2nd and 2032 Pistons 2nd)

item.title: Last year is a team option
item.description: Bobby Marks: Stewart has 2 years left: $15M in each season Last year is a team option 2 ways to acquire Stewart - non tax ML or; - trade exception

item.title: Ja Morant super available in trade talks
item.description: NBA Base: Ja Morant is “flagrantly available” in trade talks, but his 2-year contract is making teams hesitant to take on the risk, per @WindhorstESPN "I just can't see him playing for the Grizzlies again." (Via @UnSportsESPN)

item.title: Tracy: “That's what I feel. I don't think it's a bad …
item.description: Tracy: “If you have a center like Zack Edey, who is slow-footed on the defensive side of the ball, you need your power forward to be able to cover some ground for him defensively. I think you could have two guys in your front court that are slow-footed, can't really cover that much ground. So, when you're playing a team like OKC or San Antonio, who can spread you out with shooters. They can't cover that ground.” Vince: “You need more of an athletic big for that pick. So, Caleb Wilson fits that m

item.title: Iowa’s Brendan Hausen will join the Memphis Grizzlies …
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Iowa’s Brendan Hausen will join the Memphis Grizzlies for NBA Summer League, sources tell DraftExpress. The 6’4” long-range shooter hit 200 threes in his NCAA career while maintaining a 38.3 3P% and 84.6 FT%. Left a lasting impression at his pre-draft workout in Memphis.

item.title: Zach Cleveland has signed an Exhibit 10 contract with …
item.description: Liberty Men’s Basketball: Zach Cleveland has signed an Exhibit 10 contract with the @Memphis Grizzlies!

item.title: Cam Boozer said he chose number 27 because his mom …
item.description: Damichael Cole: Cam Boozer said he chose number 27 because his mom wore it in college when she played college volleyball

item.title: GG Jackson is here at the introductory press …
item.description: Drew Hill: GG Jackson is here at the introductory press conference for the newest Grizzlies.

item.title: Timberwolves explored James Harden, Ja Morant as point guard options
item.description: At the same time, teams like Minnesota and Toronto — franchises that had been extremely aggressive in pursuing upgrades — found themselves with an opportunity to improve their fortunes. The Wolves, after sending Julius Randle to Brooklyn, were left with a $33 million trade exception and a cap book $66 million leaner, while continuing to signal intentions to add a quality point guard, sources say (a pursuit that at one time included Cleveland's James Harden and Memphis' Ja Morant). The Raptors, w

item.title: ESPN and ABC's broadcast of the 2026 NBA Draft first …
item.description: ESPN and ABC's broadcast of the 2026 NBA Draft first round averaged 4.2 million viewers Tuesday night, a 10% increase from the prior year, the network announced. The audience peaked at 5.5 million viewers during the 8:15 p.m. ET quarter hour. AJ Dybantsa went first overall to the Washington Wizards, followed by Darryn Peterson to the Utah Jazz at No. 2, Cameron Boozer to the Memphis Grizzlies at No. 3, Caleb Wilson to the Chicago Bulls at No. 4 and Keaton Wagler to the Los Angeles Clippers at No

item.title: Ja Morant expected to be traded this week
item.description: Sources say the Heat had interest in Grizzlies point guard Ja Morant before the Giannis deal, but there is not much expectation that he will get bought out. In fact, league sources expect Morant to be traded this week.

item.title: Cameron Boozer: Memphis is going to really surprise people this year
item.description: Cameron Boozer on the Grizzlies' potential for the upcoming season: "We are a team who is going to really surprise people this year. We are going to be shockingly good to people. I think people don't really understand that just because obviously it was kind of a rebuild, but you look at the players we have, the roster who we are bringing in this year, and I think we are going to have something pretty special."

item.title: Cameron Boozer: "I don't cheat the game, I show up at …
item.description: Cameron Boozer: "I don't cheat the game, I show up at work, I have high energy and I think it starts with great coaching. I have always been surrounded by great coaches and players who really want to buy in and win. For me, I was always the best player on my team doing the right things, and it just trickles down and everyone starts doing the right things. People talk about my winning, but you can't do that without a team."

item.title: Teams want draft compensation in order to pull the trigger on Ja Morant trade
item.description: The Memphis Grizzlies have been open to dealing Morant since the trade deadline, and in the past few days their asking price has dropped considerably from draft capital before last week's draft to pick swaps. Now they're now trying to match player for player in deals. Teams have asked the Grizzlies for draft compensation in order to take on Morant, so the gulf has been considerable, especially with so many point guards going early in Round 1 of the draft.

item.title: GG Jackson, Olivier-Maxence Prosper returning to Memphis as team picks up their player options
item.description: Jake Fischer: The same goes for Memphis and GG Jackson’s $2.4 million club option.

item.title: The Grizzlies are picking up the $2.5 million team …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Grizzlies are picking up the $2.5 million team option for Olivier-Maxence Prosper, according to league sources.

item.title: Kings had, before they drafted Darius Acuff, they had …
item.description: Sam Amick: I hate always talking, we talk about players like assets and that is just part of the economy of the game, but you constantly hear with Ja that he remains a negative value asset, that that is the way he is perceived. And so that means when teams are calling, and I have shared this before, like in my backyard, the Kings had, before they drafted Darius Acuff, they had legitimate interest in Ja (Morant), but it was always with the disclaimer that they wanted draft assets attached to him

item.title: “It doesn’t have to be glamorous if it works,” Coward …
item.description: Shortly afterwards, the Grizzlies acquired Coward in a pre-draft trade with Portland as last year’s No. 11 pick. Coward then made the NBA’s All-Rookie First team for his positional versatility, team-oriented play and efficiency. That journey validated why Coward became intrigued with joining an independent sports agency. “It doesn’t have to be glamorous if it works,” Coward said. “As an agency, I think we’re growing each and every other day. [Ramasar’s] putting as much into this as he puts into

item.title: Grizzlies keep GG Jackson, Olivier-Maxence Prosper for next season
item.description: Grizzlies PR: The @Memphis Grizzlies today announced the team exercised 2026-27 contract options on forwards GG Jackson II and Olivier-Maxence Prosper.

item.title: 2026 First-round pick Bennett Stirtz: 'I thought I was going to be Division II my whole career'
item.description: During the pre-NBA Draft process, Bennett Stirtz worked out with the Chicago Bulls, Oklahoma City Thunder, Golden State Warriors, Memphis Grizzlies, Toronto Raptors and others. Now he’s in New York, getting ready for his name to be called at the 2026 NBA Draft. A few years ago, not even Stirtz thought this was possible. “Not at all,” Stirtz said. “I thought I was going to be Division II my whole career.”

item.title: Bennett Stirtz grabs a Grizzlies hat as he walks onto …
item.description: Bennett Stirtz grabs a Grizzlies hat as he walks onto the stage to shake Silver’s hand and pose for a photo. But there’s a twist. Stirtz was alerted shortly before the pick that the Grizzlies would be trading him to his actual landing spot, the Oklahoma City Thunder. “My agent — it was probably like a minute before Memphis picked — he's like, ‘You're going to OKC,’” Stirtz said. “Before anyone else knew, I knew.” McCollum called Stirtz and the Thunder a “perfect fit.”

item.title: Ja Morant heads to Portland for Jerami Grant, Kris Murray
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The Memphis Grizzlies are trading two-time NBA All-Star Ja Morant to the Portland Trail Blazers for Jerami Grant and Kris Murray, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: The Sacramento Kings were eyeing the possibility of …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: The Sacramento Kings were eyeing the possibility of adding Ja Morant dating back to the last NBA trade deadline, but drafting Darius Acuff, Jr., recently cooled that talk.

item.title: Ja Morant has played 79 total games over the last 3 …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Ja Morant has played 79 total games over the last 3 seasons, hasn't played in 7 straight games for the Grizzlies since the 2022-23 season.

item.title: Contract details for Memphis/Portland
item.description: Bobby Marks: Contract details for Memphis/Portland To Portland Ja Morant 2026/27: $42.2M 2027/28: $44.9M To Memphis Jerami Grant 2026/27: $34.2M 2027/28: $37.2M (P) Kris Murray 2026/27: $5.3M

item.title: Ja Morant has a 15 percent trade bonus. It would be …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Ja Morant has a 15 percent trade bonus. It would be voided if the trade is executed today or tomorrow. If done on July 6, he would be entitled to a $13 million trade bonus. It would increase his $42.2 million salary to $48.7 million. Jerami Grant ($34.2 million) and Kris Murray ($5.3 million) matches for Morant on that $48.7 million salary. We'll see if he gets the entire amount or waives it.

item.title: As a result of the trade happening today, he will not …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Ja Morant trade is official. As a result of the trade happening today, he will not receive his trade bonus. He carries a $42.2 million cap hit and the Trail Blazers maintain more cap flexibility. They are $27.6 million under the luxury tax line with 11 players rostered.

item.title: Ja Morant excited to be in Portland
item.description: According to Shams Charania of ESPN, the embattled star guard is reportedly thrilled to welcome new beginnings in Portland. "They felt like they had to take a swing just based on the talent that Ja Morant is and hoping that he has a chip on his shoulder, motivation to turn his career around," the NBA insider said about the Blazers trading for Morant. "The Blazers did speak to Ja Morant today, shortly after the trade. I'm told he expressed to them that he's excited to be in Portland."

item.title: Portland plans on starting Morant and Damian Lillard …
item.description: ESPN's Shams Charania reported Portland acquired Ja Morant via trade from the Memphis Grizzlies in exchange for Jerami Grant and Kris Murray. According to NBA insider Chris Haynes, Portland plans on starting Morant and Damian Lillard in the backcourt alongside Deni Avdija on the wing following "a very unconventional move that the franchise believes has upside."

item.title: One executive from another team I talked to called the …
item.description: The Ja Morant trade caught many around the NBA by surprise, as the oft-injured Memphis Grizzlies guard was traded to the Portland Trail Blazers for Jerami Grant and Kris Murray. One executive from another team I talked to called the deal “confusing," citing the Blazers' glut of guards with Damian Lillard returning from Achilles surgery and Jrue Holiday and Scoot Henderson under contract. He wondered whether there was a corresponding deal the Blazers were working on to clear up the position.

item.title: The Grizzlies saved a $13 million check to Morant by …
item.description: Regarding the Ja Morant trade, there is one important reason to do the deal now rather than in July. That is because Morant is still in year seven of his career rather than year eight, which means his max is capped at 25 percent of the cap (for a player who has completed six full years of service) rather than 30 percent. That matters because Morant has a 15 percent trade kicker on his deal, which doesn't matter between now and June 30 since his 2025-26 salary is already above the six-year-of-ser

item.title: No picks changed hands in the Ja Morant trade to the …
item.description: Jake Fischer: No picks changed hands in the Ja Morant trade to the Trail Blazers, but the Memphis Grizzlies did send $1 million in cash to Portland, sources say, in exchange for Jerami Grant and Kris Murray.

item.title: That is because Morant is still in year seven of his …
item.description: Regarding the Ja Morant deal, there is one important reason to do the deal now rather than in July. That is because Morant is still in year seven of his career rather than year eight, which means his max is capped at 25 percent of the max (for a player who has completed six full years of service) rather than 30 percent.

item.title: 12, thank you for every highlight, every memory, every …
item.description: Memphis Grizzlies: 2x All-Star All-NBA Second Team Rookie of the Year All-Rookie First Team Most Improved Player Franchise record for points in a single game (52) 12, thank you for every highlight, every memory, every unforgettable moment and for all you've given this team, this community and this city. From the with love Thank you @JaMorant for 7 great years.

item.title: "This was their big move, Charania said. "As of right …
item.description: Here's what ESPN's Shams Charania had to say on "SportsCenter" when asked whether the Portland Trail Blazers could make a move for the Boston Celtics' Jaylen Brown after they traded for Ja Morant on Monday: "This was their big move, Charania said. "As of right now, my understanding is they're not involved with Jaylen Brown."

item.title: Donovan Clingan off limits in trade talks
item.description: One thing has become certain about the Trail Blazers after Monday’s trade for point guard Ja Morant: Portland center Donovan Clingan is now essentially off limits in trade talks, according to a team source.

item.title: Ten years ago today, I spent a quarter-billion dollars …
item.description: John Hollinger: Ten years ago today, I spent a quarter-billion dollars of somebody else’s money in less than an hour. Well, it wasn’t me, exactly, but as a member of the Memphis Grizzlies’ front office, I certainly had a prominent role in it. In contrast to the crawl into free agency that we’ve seen in 2026, where “pre-agency” took many of the best players off the table before they ever hit the market and most of the rest of the good ones are also re-signing with their own teams, NBA free agency

item.title: To give you an idea of where things stood: We were …
item.description: If you weren’t willing to fork out in the high eight figures for another team’s ninth-best player, five other teams were lined up behind you, ready to do it. And this was in 2016 money — with max contracts below $30 million — not 2026 money! The first announced deal came just at the stroke of midnight on the East Coast, when Timofey Mozgov was reported to be going to the Los Angeles Lakers for four years and $64 million. Things quickly degenerated from there. To give you an idea of where things

item.title: We tried to get Eric Gordon to take $40 million, since …
item.description: We had a meeting at the first possible moment — 9 p.m. in L.A. — with little-used Indiana Pacers forward Solomon Hill, and realized our hopes of getting him for “just” four years and $40 million were unrealistic given the multiple rivals lining up to beat our offer (New Orleans eventually got him for four years and $52 million). We tried to get Eric Gordon to take $40 million, since he’d known our star guard, Mike Conley, since childhood; we didn’t know he had more than $50 million waiting for h

item.title: Amazingly, two of the deals turned out well. Conley …
item.description: Amazingly, two of the deals turned out well. Conley signed what was, at the time, the richest contract in NBA history: a five-year, $153 million deal to stay with us in Memphis. He played well enough to return two first-round picks near the end of that contract. And James Ennis signed with us on a two-year, $6 million deal for our cap room exception. He ended up starting for us in the playoffs. Alas, the other deal didn’t work out so well: Chandler Parsons signed a four-year, $94 million deal wi

item.title: Grizzlies, Kentavious Caldwell-Pope expected to part ways
item.description: Chris Haynes: Memphis Grizzlies and Kentavious Caldwell-Pope are on the verge of working on a parting resolution as he could be traded or become a free agent relatively soon, league sources tell me.

item.title: Grizzlies engaged with Bucks on Ja Morant up until Monday
item.description: The Milwaukee Bucks, league sources told The Athletic, engaged the Grizzlies on potential trades for Morant as early as January, when it became known Morant was on the market. Those sources, who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe the deliberations, said discussions continued periodically up until Monday, when the Bucks chose not to go through with the trade.

item.title: Santi Aldama traded to Dallas
item.description: Shams Charania: The Memphis Grizzlies are trading Santi Aldama to the Dallas Mavericks for AJ Johnson, a protected 2030 Warriors first-round pick and two future second-round picks, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: The deal also allows the Grizzlies to preserve its …
item.description: Shams Charania: Dallas acquires a versatile forward as Memphis continues to stack draft capital. The deal also allows the Grizzlies to preserve its full $29 million trade exception from the Jaren Jackson Jr. trade, which otherwise would have been reduced by their earlier offseason dealings.

item.title: The $17M salary of Aldama goes into the $20.8M …
item.description: Bobby Marks: The $17M salary of Aldama goes into the $20.8M exception created from the Anthony Davis trade in February. Aldama has 2 years left: $17M and $17M (T) Johnson has 2 years left: $3.2M and $5.4M (T)

item.title: The Memphis Grizzlies will be able to take Isaiah …
item.description: The Memphis Grizzlies will be able to take Isaiah Stewart into the trade exception created by Santi Aldama being dealt to Dallas, which will allow a the Grizzlies to maintain their bigger $28 million trade exception from February's Jaren Jackson, Jr. trade. The Grizzlies could not take Stewart into their nontaxpayer midlevel exception even though his base salary of $15 million fits. That's because he also has $1 million in unlikely incentives that count toward that exception.

item.title: I could see the Isaiah Stewart, Isaiah Joe, John …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: I could see the Isaiah Stewart, Isaiah Joe, John Collins, & Santi Aldama trades expand into a five-team trade between the Pistons, Grizzlies, Clippers, Mavericks, & Thunder. Pistons need to send out $20M to match for Collins & Joe. Stewart ($15M) & Marcus Sasser ($5.2M) work.

item.title: Dallas Mavericks are trading AJ Johnson, a protected …
item.description: Mike Curtis: The Dallas Mavericks are trading AJ Johnson, a protected 2030 first-round pick (via Warriors) & two future second-round picks to the #Grizzlies in exchange for Santi Aldama & the draft rights to Tarik Biberovic, a person familiar with the deal confirms.

item.title: TV schedule for Salt Lake City SL has been updated:
item.description: Kevin Chouinard: TV schedule for Salt Lake City SL has been updated: Utah 7/4 UTA 5:00 ET Prime/ESPN U 7/6 OKC 7:00 ET ESPN/Prime 7/7 MEM 7:00 ET Prime/ESPN U Vegas 7/9 SAS 4:30 ET ESPN 2 7/11 BKN 8:00 ET ESPN 7/13 BOS 6:00 ET Prime 7/16 MEM 8:00 ET Prime

item.title: Bradley Beal, Anfernee Simons, Kentavious Caldwell-Pope on Miami's radar
item.description: A source said some of the bigger guards on Miami’s radar are 6-4 Bradley Beal, 6-3 Anfernee Simons and 6-5 Kentavious Caldwell Pope (who is expected to part ways with the Grizzlies). The 6-5 Zach LaVine could become an option if the Kings waive and stretch the final season (and $49 million) of his contract. And so could....

item.title: Ja Morant will wear No. 1 with the Trail Blazers next …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: Ja Morant will wear No. 1 with the Trail Blazers next season, the team announced 🔥 The new number replaces the No. 12 Morant has worn at every level since high school.

item.title: I think that's what they're looking to build off of, …
item.description: "Obviously I didn't know it was going to happen," Boozer said July 2. "Ja is a great player. It's not our decision to make. It's the front office's (decision). I think we got a great young core. I think that's what they're looking to build off of, so I think (the future) is really exciting. "Obviously it sucks to lose a guy as good as Ja, what he's done for the city and the team, but it's part of the business."

item.title: 76ers agree to sign Rayan Rupert to a two-way contract
item.description: Shams Charania: The Philadelphia 76ers have agreed to sign French guard Rayan Rupert on a two-way NBA deal, sources tell ESPN. Rupert, a second-round pick in 2023, split last season in Memphis and Portland.

item.title: For various reasons (injury/contractual, mostly), …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: RJ Barrett, Zach Edey, Dwight Powell and Kelly Olynyk are here watching Canada-Puerto Rico. For various reasons (injury/contractual, mostly), they're not playing this summer but maintain spots in the country's player pool. Expected to be in the mix for the World Cup next summer.

item.title: Final: Grizzlies 111 OKC 74
item.description: Damichael Cole: Final: Grizzlies 111 OKC 74 15-4-4 for Cam Boozer in 22 minutes. O-Max led Memphis with 17 points. Taylor Hendricks had 15 points in 12 minutes. Javon Small had 11 points and nine assists. Cedric Coward had 11 points and eight boards. Jahmai Mashack was impressive.

item.title: Brooks Barnhizer: 13 points and 6 rebounds
item.description: Justin Martinez: Final: Memphis 111, OKC 74 Brooks Barnhizer: 13 points and 6 rebounds Aday Mara: 10 points and 4 assists Bennett Stirtz: 10 points and 3 steals Cameron Boozer recorded 15 points and 4 assists for the Grizzlies.

item.title: Karim Lopez out for Salt Lake City Summer League
item.description: Drew Hill: Per team spokesperson, the Grizzlies will have full SL roster available today, other than Karim Lopez (mild LT hamstring strain during predraft process) and Walter Clayton Jr. (mild RT knee sprain), who are out for SLC summer league, with a reevaluation to come for Vegas.

item.title: Obviously, having Zubac on the team and being able to …
item.description: You played with Zach Edey at Purdue, and now you’re playing with Ivica Zubac in Indiana. They’re both traditional back-to-the-basket, post-up guys, which is rare in today’s league. What aspects of your game do you feel translate well from when you played with Edey to now, hopefully with Zubac? Braden Smith: Obviously, two big dudes and being able to play with those guys and learn how to play with them. I think it was important for me, especially early on in my first two years of college, to be w

item.title: Quinten Post signing three-year, $30 million offer sheet with Grizzlies
item.description: Shams Charania: Restricted free agent Quinten Post is signing a three-year, $30 million contract offer sheet with the Memphis Grizzlies, sources tell ESPN. The Golden State Warriors have until 11:59pm ET on Tuesday to match.

item.title: George Roussakis and Mark Bartelstein of …
item.description: Shams Charania: George Roussakis and Mark Bartelstein of @PrioritySports landed the offer sheet for Post on Monday morning. The 7-footer averaged 7.8 points, 3.8 rebounds and 36.4% 3-point shooting across his first two NBA seasons with the Warriors, who selected him No. 52 overall in 2024.

item.title: This contract for Quentin Post will go into the …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: This contract for Quentin Post will go into the Grizzlies mid-level exception. If matched, it’ll be replenished for different moves. If unmatched, they’ll need to acquire Isaiah Stewart with the big $29 million trade exception or by expanding the Santi Aldama trade.

item.title: I suspect the Warriors won’t match as they need to …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: I suspect the Warriors won’t match as they need to maintain cap flexibility for other potential moves.

item.title: The Warriors have been bracing for a Quinten Post …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Warriors have been bracing for a Quinten Post offer sheet for several days, source say, while Golden State remains entrenched in the LeBron James free agency sweepstakes.

item.title: Last time a player signed an offer sheet of $5M+ per …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Some context on the Quinten Post offer sheet in Memphis. * Last time a player signed an offer sheet of $5M+ per season was Matisse Thybulle with Dallas in 2023. * Last time an offer sheet went unmatched was 2020 (Bogdan Bogdanovic with Sacramento/Atlanta)

item.title: Everything I’ve heard is that the Edey trajectory …
item.description: Chris Herrington: Everything I’ve heard is that the Edey trajectory hasn’t changed. Expectation is he’ll be ready for opening night. But I also expect they’ll be careful with him. He’ll miss games. So will Stewart. They needed a third center.

item.title: Only the first season of Quinten Post’s three-year, …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Only the first season of Quinten Post’s three-year, $30 million offer sheet from Memphis is guaranteed, per sources. Yet there are ~$1.3M in unlikely incentives each season if Post were to make an All-Defense Team, so that extra money would count against the Warriors’ apron

item.title: It was Peterson who stole the show with 25 points and …
item.description: But it’s hard to watch Darryn Peterson’s first two games in the Salt Lake City Summer League and not think he can be truly special. Monday night, in a game hyped as No. 2 pick Peterson against No. 3 pick Cameron Boozer, it was Peterson who stole the show with 25 points and 12 assists (to just two turnovers). He was explosive, showed off his shooting range, and his decision-making was way beyond his years. “What is greatness to me? I think it’s just kind of being one-of-one,” Peterson said. “I wo

item.title: Taylor Hendricks is out tonight.
item.description: Damichael Cole: Per team spokesperson: Taylor Hendricks is out tonight. He’s considered day to day,. The rest of the Grizzlies from the last game are available again tonight. Walter Clayton Jr. and Karim Lopez remain out

item.title: The trade to make him a Maverick is still pending, but …
item.description: Marc Stein: UPDATE: The trade to make him a Maverick is still pending, but league sources say Tarik Biberović has informed Fenerbahçe he’s leaving for the NBA. After acquiring him from Memphis, Dallas is expected to sign Biberović to a two-year, $6 million deal with a team option in Year 2.

item.title: Dallas Hoops Journal has learned Tarik Biberovic is …
item.description: Dallas Hoops Journal has learned Tarik Biberovic is expected to leave Fenerbahçe and join the Mavericks once his buyout is finalized, ending an eight-season run in Turkey. Under NBA rules, Dallas can only cover $900,000 of the $2M buyout required for him to depart, leaving Biberović to cover the rest.

item.title: The Dallas Mavericks are expected to add one of …
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks are expected to add one of Europe’s top shooters. Dallas Hoops Journal has learned Tarik Biberović is expected to complete a buyout of his Fenerbahçe contract and join Dallas on a multi-year deal, ending an eight-season run in Turkey and setting up his arrival in the NBA this fall.

item.title: Dallas Hoops Journal has learned the two sides have …
item.description: Biberović signed a new three-year contract with Fenerbahçe on July 10, 2025, turning down interest from Memphis to stay in Istanbul. That deal carried a $2M buyout clause allowing him to leave for the NBA, and under league rules, Dallas could only contribute up to $900K toward it, leaving Biberović responsible for the rest. Dallas Hoops Journal has learned the two sides have worked through that gap, with Fenerbahçe likely to receive the full buyout amount to complete the move. That process s

item.title: Dallas is sending Middleton to the Wizards via …
item.description: Shams Charania: Dallas is sending Middleton to the Wizards via sign-and-trade as part of a larger six-team deal that includes, in total, the Mavericks, Wizards, Clippers, Pistons, Bucks and Grizzlies. It involves previously agreed upon deals such as John Collins, Gary Harris and Taurean Prince (Detroit), Isaiah Stewart (Memphis), Santi Aldama (Mavericks), Caris LeVert (Bucks), etc.

item.title: D'Angelo Russell heads to Memphis
item.description: Shams Charania: As part of the elaborate six-team trade, the Washington Wizards are sending D'Angelo Russell, a future second-round pick and a future second-round pick swap to the Memphis Grizzlies, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Because of the Khris Middleton sign-and-trade, Dallas …
item.description: Bobby Marks: A nice job by the Mavericks front office on this deal. Because of the Khris Middleton sign-and-trade, Dallas will preserve the big $20.8M Anthony Davis trade exception. To break it down on a smaller scale: Middleton/AJ Johnson for Santi Aldama

item.title: Memphis can take on Stewart's $15 million salary in …
item.description: Memphis also gets to keep its massive $28.9 million traded player exception. The TPE was gained from the Feb. 3 trade that resulted in Jaren Jackson Jr. going to the Utah Jazz. Memphis can take on Stewart's $15 million salary in the bigger deal and part ways with Aldama's $17 million without touching the TPE.

item.title: The Golden State Warriors decided not to match Quinten …
item.description: ‪Shams Charania: The Golden State Warriors decided not to match Quinten Post's three-year, $30 million offer sheet with the Grizzlies, allowing the 7-footer to join Memphis, sources tell ESPN. The Grizzlies add Post to a reshaped frontline that includes No. 3 pick Cameron Boozer, Zach Edey, Isaiah Stewart and

item.title: First time since 2020 that an offer sheet of $5M plus …
item.description: Bobby Marks: First time since 2020 that an offer sheet of $5M plus goes unmatched. However, the Bogdan Bogdanovic (Sac to Atl) unmatched sheet was during the 2017 CBA and different set of rules.

item.title: “No matter where you send me I’m still dangerous”
item.description: Fullcourtpass: D’Angelo Russell on the Wizards trading him to the Grizzlies “No matter where you send me I’m still dangerous”

item.title: Stein‬: I'm told that six-team trade involving the …
item.description: ‪Marc Stein‬: I'm told that six-team trade involving the Pistons, Clippers, Grizzlies, Bucks, Wizards and Mavericks is on course to be made official and announced shortly. More NBA from @jakelfischer.bsky.social and me: tinyurl.com/5b4w9jws

item.title: Per the NBA’s transaction wire, a lot of Pistons …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Per the NBA’s transaction wire, a lot of Pistons trades are now official. They’ve acquired John Collins, Taurean Prince and Gary Harris and traded Isaiah Stewart, Caris LeVert and Marcus Sasser.

item.title: Six-team trade involving Mavs, Pistons, Clippers, Grizzlies, Bucks and Wizards made official
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks announced today their participation in a six-team trade involving the Detroit Pistons, L.A. Clippers, Memphis Grizzlies, Milwaukee Bucks and Washington Wizards. Dallas acquired forward Santi Aldama and the rights to forward Tarik Biberović from Memphis along with guard Marcus Sasser from Detroit. Dallas traded guard AJ Johnson, a top-20 protected 2030 first-round pick (via Golden State) and a 2029 second-round pick (via Houston) to Memphis. The Mavericks also sent forward K

item.title: The @Memphis Grizzlies today announced the team …
item.description: Grizzlies PR: The @Memphis Grizzlies today announced the team acquired Isaiah Stewart, AJ Johnson, D’Angelo Russell, a conditional future first round draft pick, three future second round draft picks and a future second round draft pick swap in a six-team trade.

item.title: The @Memphis Grizzlies today announced the team signed …
item.description: Grizzlies PR: The @Memphis Grizzlies today announced the team signed Quinten Post to a multi-year contract.

item.title: "You tell him something, he does it right away," …
item.description: George's single-game assignment would have been symbolic of the mentoring relationship he has instantly developed with Peterson. George offers advice to Peterson on some of the finer points of NBA guard play, pleased to help his new long-term backcourt partner take his first steps toward fulfilling his immense potential. "You tell him something, he does it right away," George told ESPN before watching Peterson's 25-point, 12-assist performance during Utah's win over No. 3 pick Cameron Boozer and

item.title: Warriors bring back Charles Bassey
item.description: Shams Charania: The Golden State Warriors and center Charles Bassey have agreed on a one-year deal, bringing the big man back to the Bay Area for his sixth NBA season, sources tell ESPN. Bassey returns to Golden State in wake of Quinten Post joining the Grizzlies on an offer sheet.

item.title: The Dallas Mavericks announced today that they have …
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks announced today that they have signed forward Tarik Biberovic (TAH-rick bee-BEHR-oh-vitch). Per team policy, terms of the deal were not disclosed. Dallas acquired the rights to Biberović along with forward Santi Aldama from Memphis as part of a six-team trade that was announced on Wednesday. The Mavericks sent guard AJ Johnson, a top-20 protected 2030 first-round pick (via Golden State) and a 2029 second-round pick (via Houston) to the Grizzlies as part of the deal.

item.title: “I cried before I played today,” the Chicago Bulls …
item.description: “I cried before I played today,” the Chicago Bulls rookie said after tallying 35 points, five boards and three blocks in his Las Vegas Summer League debut, a 97-96 loss to the Memphis Grizzlies. “It’s been five months to the day since the last time I played, and I’ve just been really emotional because I haven’t been able to play. I felt terrible because my team lost in the tournament and my coach got fired. It was a lot for me at that point. “So coming out today, it just felt like I’ve been wait

item.title: "I'm definitely grateful for my time in Memphis," he …
item.description: Drew Hill: Ja Morant is welcoming a fresh start. He said he wants to show Portland a "different Ja." But that reality is still sinking in. "I'm definitely grateful for my time in Memphis," he said. "That will always be home for me."

item.title: Ja Morant still adjusting to life with the Portland …
item.description: Ben Golliver: Ja Morant still adjusting to life with the Portland Trail Blazers after the trade from the Memphis Grizzlies. “Oh my God, I signed with the number 12!”

item.title: Besiktas Istanbul have announced another addition …
item.description: Besiktas Istanbul have announced another addition ahead of their long-awaited return to the EuroLeague next season. The club officially confirmed the signing of former NBA guard Jaylen Nowell. The 27-year-old has appeared in more than 200 NBA games, representing the Minnesota Timberwolves, Memphis Grizzlies, Detroit Pistons, and New Orleans Pelicans, among other teams.

item.title: The Golden State Warriors signed Charles Bassey to a …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Golden State Warriors signed Charles Bassey to a one-year, $2.85 million deal, which is partially guaranteed for $1.4 million, league sources told @hoopshype . Bassey helps fill the void of Quinten Post, who recently signed with the Grizzlies. ESPN first reported an agreement.

item.title: Grizzlies officially sign No. 3 draft pick Cameron Boozer
item.description: Grizzlies PR: The @Memphis Grizzlies today announced the team signed Cameron Boozer to a multi-year contract.
